name: optimusprime
description: The session state protocol for AI coding. Hook-level enforcement, cross-session memory, predictive intelligence, and agentic conductor.

commands.name: op-watch
commands.description: Show live OptimusPrime status — tokens, cost, decisions, loops, compression, active Auto Bots. Runs entirely inside Claude Code, no terminal needed.
commands.prompt:
Show the current OptimusPrime status dashboard. Walk up from the current directory to find .optimusprime/ (check cwd, parent, grandparent up to 10 levels). Read these files and display the dashboard:

1. .optimusprime/contract.json → goal, complexity_budget
2. .optimusprime/cost-log.json → last session token_estimate and estimated_cost_usd
3. .optimusprime/decisions.md → count lines containing DECIDED or DECISION
4. .optimusprime/loop-state.json → length of consecutive_failures array
5. .optimusprime/compression-log.json → average of all ratio values (it is a list)
6. .optimusprime/skills.json → skills with mode auto or always

Display exactly this format:
```
⚡ OPTIMUSPRIME — ACTIVE
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
🎯 Goal:        [goal or "no contract yet"]
💬 Tokens:      [n]k  (~$[cost:.4f])
📝 Decisions:   [n] logged
🔁 Loop streak: [n]  [add ⚠️ if 2 or more]
📊 Compression: [avg]%  [or "no data"]
🤖 Active Bots: [comma list or "standby"]
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Hooks firing: pre-response ✓ | scope-guard ✓ | loop-detector ✓ | compressor ✓
```

If .optimusprime/ does not exist anywhere in the directory tree: run mkdir -p .optimusprime in the current directory and display: ✓ OptimusPrime initialized — .optimusprime/ created. Now re-read (all files will be empty/missing — show zeroes).

commands.name: op-autopilot
commands.description: Pre-session brief — what was done last session, where you left off, failed approaches to avoid, open TODOs, and recommended next step. No terminal needed.
commands.prompt:
Show an OptimusPrime pre-session brief. Find .optimusprime/ by walking up from cwd. Read:
1. session-snapshot.md → last session summary, goal, open threads, next_action
2. contract.json → goal and complexity_budget
3. attempts.md → failed approaches (list them so we avoid repeating)
4. todos.md → pending TODOs

Display:
```
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
⚡ OPTIMUSPRIME AUTOPILOT BRIEF
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
📋 LAST SESSION: [summary or "no snapshot"]
🎯 CURRENT GOAL: [goal or "no contract"]
⚠️  AVOID (failed): [from attempts.md, or "none"]
📌 OPEN TODOS: [from todos.md, or "none"]
➡️  RECOMMENDED NEXT: [from snapshot next_action]
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
```

If .optimusprime/ does not exist: create it with mkdir -p .optimusprime and say 'No previous session — starting fresh. OptimusPrime initialized.'

commands.name: op-snapshot
commands.description: View the current session snapshot — goal, files changed, decisions made, open threads, next action. This is exactly what gets injected at the start of the next session.
commands.prompt: Show the current OptimusPrime session snapshot. Find .optimusprime/ by walking up from cwd. Read session-snapshot.md and display its full content clearly formatted. If session-snapshot.md does not exist, synthesize a snapshot from available data: read contract.json (goal), decisions.md (last 5 decisions), cost-log.json (tokens), and display what is available. If .optimusprime/ does not exist, create it and say 'No snapshot yet — session logger will write one at end of session.'

commands.name: op-decisions
commands.description: View the last 10 decisions logged for this project — what was chosen, what was rejected, and why.
commands.prompt: Show the last 10 OptimusPrime decisions. Find .optimusprime/decisions.md by walking up from cwd. Read the file and show the last 10 lines that contain DECIDED or DECISION. Display each with its timestamp and full text. If the file does not exist or is empty, say 'No decisions logged yet — OptimusPrime logs decisions automatically as sessions run. To manually log one, I can append to .optimusprime/decisions.md now.'

commands.name: op-intel
commands.description: Query project intelligence — ask why decisions were made, find past patterns, check contradictions.
commands.prompt: Query OptimusPrime project intelligence. Ask the user: 'What would you like to know about this project? (Examples: why did we choose X, what failed before, what is the architecture decision for Y)'. Then search .optimusprime/decisions.md for lines matching their question keywords. Also check session-snapshot.md for relevant context. Show matching results with full text and timestamps. If no .optimusprime/ found, create it and say no history yet.

commands.name: op-contract
commands.description: View or update the current scope contract — goal, in-scope files, out-of-scope files, complexity budget.
commands.prompt:
Show the current OptimusPrime scope contract. Find .optimusprime/contract.json by walking up from cwd. Read and display all fields clearly:
- Goal
- In-scope files
- Out-of-scope files
- Complexity budget
- Created at
- Agent ID

Then ask: 'Would you like to update any field?' If yes, update contract.json with the user's changes and confirm. If contract.json does not exist, offer to create one by asking for the session goal, then write the contract with sensible defaults.

commands.name: op-conductor
commands.description: Run autonomous multi-step tasks with full guardrails — OptimusPrime breaks your goal into subtasks, executes each, checks quality, and escalates when stuck.
commands.prompt:
Start OptimusPrime Conductor mode. Ask: 'What goal should Conductor work on autonomously?' Then:
1. Break the goal into 3-5 concrete subtasks with clear acceptance criteria
2. Display the subtask plan and ask for approval
3. After approval: execute each subtask sequentially using available tools (Read, Edit, Write, Bash)
4. After each subtask: log the outcome to .optimusprime/decisions.md as: [ISO timestamp] DECIDED: [what was done]
5. Check done criteria: does the change work, are types clean, are no regressions introduced
6. If stuck after 3 attempts on any subtask: stop and report to user with details

Find .optimusprime/ by walking up from cwd. Create it if missing. Create contract.json with the given goal if none exists.

commands.name: op-replay
commands.description: Replay any past session like a debugger — see every decision, failure, loop, and compression event in chronological order.
commands.prompt:
Replay the most recent OptimusPrime session. Find .optimusprime/ by walking up from cwd. Read:
1. decisions.md → all DECIDED lines with timestamps
2. attempts.md → all FAILED attempts
3. session-snapshot.md → final session state
4. compression-log.json → compression events with timestamps

Display all events merged in chronological order:
```
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
⚡ SESSION REPLAY
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
[time] DECISION: ...
[time] FAILED:   ...
[time] COMPRESSED: N chars removed
...
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
SUMMARY: N decisions, N failures, N% compression avg
```

If no data: say 'No session history in .optimusprime/ — history accumulates as sessions run.'
